name: Mito-transfer improved elderly human CD4 T-cell senescence markers ex vivo
summary: Ex vivo elderly-human T-cell evidence provides human-cell support but not treated-human efficacy.
statement: In aged mouse CD4+ T-cell models and ex vivo CD4+ T cells from elderly humans, mitochondrial transfer increased mitochondrial mass, altered cytokine production, improved activation/proliferation readouts, and reduced exhaustion and senescence marker patterns.
population_or_model: Aged mouse CD4+ T cells and ex vivo CD4+ T cells from elderly human donors
time_horizon: Short ex vivo and mouse infection-model follow-up
quantitative_note: The human-cell figure reports 9 to 10 biological replicates per group for selected elderly-human CD4+ T-cell assays.
caveats: The elderly-human evidence is ex vivo and should not be treated as a human intervention trial.
The mouse component is infection-context immune biology, not broad aging efficacy.
Cell-product durability, safety, and in vivo delivery remain unproven for aging applications.
